title: Yizhi Liang
text:
Research Experience
Leonard D. Schaeffer Center for Health Policy & Economics,
University of Southern California
Research Assistant
Project: Cost-Effectiveness and Budget Impact of IVIG versus Jan 2026 – Present
Corticosteroids for Moderate-to-Severe Pediatric PANS/PANDAS

Developed a Markov cohort model in R estimating ICERs and net monetary benefit for IVIG versus corticosteroids alone from Medicaid and societal perspectives, with probabilistic sensitivity and budget impact analyses.
Built a renv-locked, fully reproducible analysis pipeline with automated validation and CHEERS 2022 reporting.
Manuscript in preparation targeting Value in Health.

Project: Waiting for Future Trial or Using Real-World Evidence: Dec 2025 – Present
Trade-offs for Coverage with Evidence Development

Derived the policy-indifference bias threshold at which real-world-evidence-based coverage with evidence development equals RCT-based coverage in population expected net benefit.
Characterized the threshold using quantitative bias analysis and sensitivity analyses on timing advantage and trial cost in Python simulation.
Manuscript under review.

Project: The Differential Impact of Family-Level Multimorbidity on Aug 2024 – Present
Financial Hardship by Insurance Status: Evidence from MEPS 2018–2023

Analyzed the 2018–2023 Medical Expenditure Panel Survey (about 67,000 family-years) to estimate how family-level multimorbidity relates to financial hardship, including high out-of-pocket burden, medical debt, and cost-related barriers to care, across private, public, and uninsured coverage.
Built the primary analysis pipeline in Stata with R for publication-ready tables and figures, using survey-weighted probability models with year and panel fixed effects.
Manuscript under revision.

Project: Evaluating Policy Options for New Treatments Under Aug 2024 – Apr 2026
Uncertain External Evidence

Designed a novel conceptual diagram to compare policy options on new treatment with external evidence, from never granted without a local trial, delayed access pending a local trial, immediate access without a local trial, and immediate access while awaiting a local trial.
Developed a partitioned survival model to estimate the monetary values from the combination of sintilimab and chemotherapy or the chemotherapy only for treating first-line locally advanced or metastatic nonsquamous non-small-cell lung cancer to illustrate the conceptual diagram.
Employed Python to compute the expected value of perfect information (EVPI), the expected value of sample information (EVSI), and the expected net benefit of sampling (ENBS) using a two-level double-loop Monte Carlo approach, comparing the expected monetary benefits of four policy options.
Published in Value in Health (2026).

Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
Graduate Research Assistant
Project: Evaluating Fairness Across Machine Learning Algorithms in Aug 2023 – May 2024
Health Models Incorporating Race/Ethnicity as Predictors

Employed Python to build machine learning models to predict the risk of cardiovascular disease in U.S. Adults based on NHANES data and the risk of adverse pregnancy outcomes through U.S. live birth certificate data.
Deployed the nested resampling and cross-validation approach based on OPTUNA Bayesian framework to optimize hyperparameters for prediction models and ensure generalizability.
Examined the racial and ethnic differences in model accuracy under different scenarios, such as including race/ethnicity as predictors, building race/ethnicity-specific models, preprocessing to balance race/ethnicity observations, and post-processing predictions.

Project: Evolving Trends in Racial and Ethnic Disparities in Syphilis Sep 2023 – May 2024
Prevalence Among Pregnant Women in the United States

Built a Bayesian mathematical model in R to estimate trends in the prevalence of syphilis by race/ethnicity among pregnant women in the US between 2016 and 2023.
Investigated the racial/ethnic disparities in the prevalence of syphilis among pregnant women over the study period, quantified by a set of disparity metrics.

Project: Estimating PrEP Need Among People Who Inject Drugs (PWID) May 2023 – May 2024
in the US: An Incidence Estimation Study

Constructed a Bernoulli model in R to estimate state-level HIV risk among needle-sharing PWID in the U.S. with the multivariate sensitivity analyses conducted.

Project: Divergent Impacts on Health and Economic Equity: The Jan 2023 – May 2024
Community-Based Hypertension Care Program in Ghana

Developed a microsimulation model in R using the extended cost-effectiveness analysis framework to examine the distributional impact of a community-based hypertension management program in Ghana.
Simulated distributional health outcomes and financial outcomes by wealth quintiles with a concentration index to reveal health equity and economic equity to assist in multi-criteria decision-making.

Project: Inequalities in Access and Quality of Prenatal Care to Nov 2022 – May 2024
Racial/Ethnic Disparities in Congenital Syphilis Burden

Conducted scoping literature reviews to parameterize the mathematical model used in this project.
Led the data analysis in R on birth file data in the U.S., including data cleaning and computing prior distributions on key parameters, using bootstrapping techniques to obtain parameter values for categorical variables.

Rural Education Action Program, Stanford Center on China’s Economy and Institutions
Research Assistant
Project: Telemedicine Service Promotion in Rural China Aug 2019 – Aug 2022

Participated in a baseline survey of 167 rural townships enrolled in a telemedicine program.
Developed questionnaires, trained investigators, and ensured data quality.
Assisted in assessing online healthcare quality offered by Direct-to-Consumer telemedicine platforms in China.
Conducted the difference-in-differences method in Stata to draft the evaluation report of the program.

Project: Quality of Primary Care in Rural China Sep 2019 – Jun 2022

Used the standardized patient method and the clinic vignettes to assess the quality of primary care in the field.
Employed Stata to describe the relationship between outpatient satisfaction and received medical quality using quantile regression.

West China School of Public Health, Sichuan University
Research Assistant
Project: An Investigation on the Quality of Care in Rural China Aug 2019 – Jun 2022

Assisted in a China Medical Board (CMB) project researching the quality of care for non-communicable diseases (NCDs) in rural China.
Used standardized patient method and clinic vignettes to assess the quality of care.
Wrote an undergraduate thesis on the study of satisfaction with rural health services.

Project: Southwest Regional Natural Population Cohort Study Mar 2021 – Aug 2021

Used propensity score method and marginal structure model to explore the association between passive smoking exposure and insomnia in a plateau Tibetan population.

Peking Union Medical College and Chinese Academy of Medical Sciences
Research Assistant
Project: Vaccine Delivery Quality Improvement Interventions in Aug 2020 – Sep 2020
Elementary Schools

Conducted literature review about the social and behavioral theories on vaccine hesitancy.
Co-wrote main sections in the Project Application for the CMB project that evaluated the cost-effectiveness analysis of Flu vaccine delivery in Beijing.

title: Yizhi Liang
text:
Professional Experience
Merck
Health Economics Intern, 2026 Future Talent Program Jun 2026 – Aug 2026

Designed and built an AI-assisted quality-check workflow for Excel-based health economic models, integrating large-language-model review with a structured QC checklist grounded in ISPOR-SMDM good modeling practices.
Developed a reusable stress-test catalog and validation protocol for systematic verification of model calculations, parameter traceability, and scenario logic.

Stratevi
Summer Intern Jun 2025 – Aug 2025

Drafted internal best practice guidelines deck for designing and implementing Delphi panel studies in health research.
Drafted a proposal for a real-world evidence study utilizing indirect propensity score matching to compare treatment effects.
Created internal reference materials on pharmaceutical market access in Germany, including detailed summaries of the healthcare system, regulatory pathways, health technology assessment procedures, and drug pricing and reimbursement mechanisms.
Contributed to the development of an Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification application to the World Health Organization by proposing strategic code assignments, analyzing relevant precedents, documenting meetings, drafting the dossier, and conducting quality assurance reviews.
Built and maintained internal R and Python-based open-source model repositories to support cost-effectiveness modeling, integrate data and code sources, and validate model executability.
Assessed and validated an Excel-based economic model evaluating the clinical benefits and cost savings of a novel one-time skin treatment for acute, nonthermal, full-thickness skin defects (FTSDs) requiring autografting, from a U.S. payer perspective.
Reviewed and performed quality control on a statistical analysis plan for a longitudinal study investigating patterns of health status, drug utilization, and satisfaction among patients with COPD.

Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
Teaching Assistant Jan 2024 – May 2024

Led the course lab for the courses GHP 501 Modeling for Health System Analysis & Priority Setting and GHP 201 Advanced Modeling for Health System Analysis & Priority Setting in 2024 Spring.
Taught about R in data analysis and economic evaluation, especially the cost-effectiveness analysis and the extended cost-effectiveness analysis.

Dept. for HIV/AIDS Prevention and Control, Zhuhai Center for Disease Control and Prevention
Intern Aug 2021 – Nov 2021

Employed R/Stata to build an automatic system to generate local monthly HIV/AIDS epidemic reports based on surveillance data.
Assisted in conducting HIV/AIDS research about social-network-based HIV self-testing promotion among men who have sex with men.

West China Hospital of Sichuan University
Medical Intern Aug 2020 – Dec 2020

Engaged in the daily screening and diagnosis of common diseases.
Assisted in conducting health education.

title: Yizhi Liang
text:
Leadership
Global Health and Wellness Research Association of Sichuan University
President of the Association Sep 2020 – Jun 2021

Organized six expert lectures on statistical package applications, academic frontiers, etc.
Assisted members in submitting Student-Led Research and Innovation Programs to Sichuan University on behalf of the association.

text:

Analytical Methods: Cost-effectiveness Analysis, Value-of-Information Analysis, Causal Inference in Real-World Data, Bayesian Mathematical Modeling, Machine Learning
Software & Tools: TreeAge, Excel-based health economic modeling, Git, AI-assisted research workflows (LLM-based model QC)

title: Yizhi Liang
text:
PhD Student in Health Economics
Alfred E. Mann School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences, University of Southern California
I am a PhD student in Health Economics at the Alfred E. Mann School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences, University of Southern California. My research spans health economics, health policy, and health services research, with a focus on health-economic modeling, real-world evidence and causal inference, and financial protection against the costs of illness.


Interests

Health-economic modeling (cost-effectiveness & value of information)
Real-world evidence & causal inference
Financial protection & health equity
AI-assisted HEOR workflows



Education

PhD in Health Economics, in progress
University of Southern California
MS in Global Health and Population, 2024
Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
MBBS in Public Health · BMgmt in Financial Management, 2022
Sichuan University
